emergent_themes.description: Health professionals experiencing conflict between professional knowledge and personal convenience-driven behavior
Deliberate cancellation of subscriptions due to fear of encouraging excessive ordering despite recognizing value
Specific academic schedule pressures (evening courses, work-study balance) driving delivery usage
Pre-planned ordering based on anticipated cravings and social occasions

coding_summary: P017 represents a fascinating case of professional-personal tension - a qualified dietitian who openly acknowledges being 'more of an eating out person' than a cook. Her ordering behavior is primarily driven by time pressure from juggling full-time work with part-time postgraduate study. Key patterns include: deliberate self-regulation (cancelling Deliveroo Plus despite value), platform loyalty with €5 switching threshold, strong preference for convenience over price, and academic schedule as primary ordering trigger. The diary data reveals craving-driven anticipatory ordering and routine convenience eating patterns. Her professional identity creates internal conflict but she's learned to compartmentalize rather than change behavior.

coverage_gaps.reason: P017 lives alone and orders solo frequently - no stigma evident, just practical behavior
